mechanism_narrative: A rapid, sustained increase in the unemployment rate over a quarter signals a definitive entry into recession, leading to broad-based earnings revisions and a decline in equity markets as investors price in a severe economic contraction.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by > 0.4pp over the last 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:The Sahm Rule uses rising unemployment as a real-time recession indicator.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when the trigger condition is met over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: Aggressive and sustained cuts to the federal funds rate signal a strong central bank commitment to supporting the economy and financial assets. This lowers the equity risk premium and discount rates, driving positive equity returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS has decreased by at least 100bps over the last 6 months
evidence_refs: fact:The Federal Reserve began cutting rates aggressively in late 2007.
failure_condition: The average 20-day S&P 500 return following periods of aggressive rate cuts is not statistically different from the long-term average (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The ongoing collapse in new residential construction, proxied by housing starts, has severe negative wealth and consumption effects that spill over into the broader economy, depressing corporate earnings and equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts have fallen more than 50% from their 2006 peak.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between the 12-month change in HOUST and 60-day forward S&P 500 returns is positive over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A negative monthly change in industrial production indicates a contraction in the manufacturing and production sectors of the economy. This is a strong coincident indicator of recession and leads to immediate negative revisions in earnings expectations.
failure_condition: The average 20-day S&P 500 return is positive in months where the INDPRO change was negative.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation remains elevated while unemployment is rising, it signals a stagflationary shock. This constrains the Federal Reserve's ability to ease policy, increasing uncertainty and risk premia, which is highly negative for equities.
candidate_trigger: Year-over-year CPIAUCSL > 3.5% AND UNRATE has increased over the last 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:The 1970s stagflation was a prolonged bear market for equities.
failure_condition: In periods matching the trigger condition, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive more than 40% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in retail sales growth is a high-frequency indicator of weakening consumer spending, which directly impacts revenues for a large portion of the S&P 500 and leads to negative short-term price adjustments.
failure_condition: The rank information coefficient between the 3-month change in RSAFS and 5-day forward S&P 500 returns is non-negative over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The first negative monthly print in nonfarm payrolls after a multi-year expansion is a powerful signal that the business cycle has turned. This regime shift triggers a rapid de-risking by investors, causing a sharp decline in equity prices.
candidate_trigger: Month-over-month change in PAYEMS < 0 for the first time in 24 months
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS turned negative in Jan 2008, confirming recession fears.
failure_condition: The average 20-day S&P 500 return following the trigger event is non-negative across historical business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A growing divergence where real GDP growth (long-term demand) slows while industrial production (short-term supply) remains firm indicates an involuntary inventory build. This foreshadows future production cuts and earnings misses, leading to negative returns.
candidate_trigger: Quarter-over-quarter growth of INDPRO > Quarter-over-quarter growth of GDPC1
evidence_refs: fact:Inventory cycles are a classic driver of business cycle volatility.
failure_condition: A strategy of shorting the S&P 500 for 60 days when the trigger is active fails to produce a positive information ratio over a full business cycle.
